canonical_question: How do I validate willingness to pay — Van Westendorp, Gabor-Granger, fake doors, constraint: only count credit cards/LOIs?
aliases: Van Westendorp Price Sensitivity Meter for startups
fake door test with payment validation
smoke test pricing with credit card capture
LOI collection for B2B pricing validation

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Stripe Payment Links and Typeform payment questions now support Apple Pay and Google Pay — broadens fake door payment capture options (2025)

constraints: Only credit card charges, pre-order payments, or signed LOIs count as validated willingness to pay — survey responses alone are directional, not proof
Van Westendorp and Gabor-Granger require minimum 100 respondents per segment for statistically meaningful results
Fake door pages must clearly disclose that the product is not yet available — FTC and consumer protection laws prohibit deceptive pre-sales
Refund all charges promptly if the product does not ship — Stripe disputes and chargebacks damage payment processing reputation
B2B LOIs are non-binding unless explicitly stated otherwise — treat them as strong intent signals, not contracts

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs a pricing strategy framework, not execution of validation research
User needs to set final pricing tiers, not validate willingness to pay
User already has validated pricing and needs to build a landing page
User needs conjoint analysis with 10+ attributes — requires enterprise tooling

inputs_needed.question: Which pricing research method should be used?
options: fake door / smoke test (validate with real money)
full sequence (survey first, then fake door)

inputs_needed.question: What is the user's technical skill level?
How many target customers can be reached?

depends_on.label: Value-based pricing strategy that defines what to validate
Product-market fit context for pricing validation

related_to.label: Dynamic pricing strategies informed by WTP research
Freemium vs paid decision using WTP thresholds

alternative_to.label: Enterprise pricing with full conjoint analysis — higher complexity
